channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Immigrant detainees sue over 'horrific' conditions at Texas ICE facility
item.description: Immigrant detainees accuse the federal government of 'inhumane' abuse and treatment at Camp East Montana in El Paso, Texas.
item.content:encoded:
Immigrant detainees accuse the federal government of 'inhumane' abuse and treatment at Camp East Montana in El Paso, Texas.

(Image credit: Morgan Lee)
item.dc:creator: Kristin Wright

item.title: Judge agrees to review Trump's $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund
item.description: The fund has sparked a broad backlash since it was announced earlier this month, exposing fractures within the Republican Party.
item.content:encoded:
The fund has sparked a broad backlash since it was announced earlier this month, exposing fractures within the Republican Party.

(Image credit: China Pool)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Boston's Brazilian residents prepare to cheer on their home country in World Cup
item.description: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S. And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
item.content:encoded: Massachusetts has one of the largest Brazilian populations in the U.S. And all across the state, Brazilians are gearing up to watch their homeland's national team compete in the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Esteban Bustillos

item.title: Strategies Americans are using for eating and shopping as food prices skyrocket
item.description: In the NPR series "What's Eating America," reporter Joe Hernandez examined how Americans across are adapting to high food prices and the strategies they use to cover them.
item.content:encoded: In the NPR series "What's Eating America," reporter Joe Hernandez examined how Americans across are adapting to high food prices and the strategies they use to cover them.
item.dc:creator: Gurjit Kaur

item.title: Pride celebrations struggle as corporate sponsorships dry up
item.description: Public support for the LGBTQ+ community by corporations has become politically risky, public relations expert says.
item.content:encoded:
Public support for the LGBTQ+ community by corporations has become politically risky, public relations expert says.

(Image credit: Hannah Frances Johansson)
item.dc:creator: Hannah Frances Johansson

item.title: Meet digital natives who collect vintage paper items at Allentown Paper Show
item.description: We have less need for paper than past generations, but paper ephemera is still very collectible. We meet some digital natives who collect old postcards and comics at a paper show in Pennsylvania.
item.content:encoded: We have less need for paper than past generations, but paper ephemera is still very collectible. We meet some digital natives who collect old postcards and comics at a paper show in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Buffy Gorrilla

item.title: Former ambassador Ivo Daalder on his critique of the U.S.' approach to military conflict
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon asks Ivo Daalder, former U.S. ambassador to NATO, about his recent criticism of America's approach to military conflict.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon asks Ivo Daalder, former U.S. ambassador to NATO, about his recent criticism of America's approach to military conflict.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Hegseth urges Asian leaders to boost military spending against China
item.description: The U.S. defense secretary said there is "rightful alarm" of China's military build up. But he also struck a more moderate tone on U.S.-China relations – and notably sidestepped bringing up Taiwan.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. defense secretary said there is "rightful alarm" of China's military build up. But he also struck a more moderate tone on U.S.-China relations – and notably sidestepped bringing up Taiwan.

(Image credit: Anupam Nath)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: The NTSB tries to keep cockpit audio recordings private. AI is making that harder
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board temporarily pulled its docket system offline after digital images were used to reconstruct cockpit voice recordings of the pilots in a recent crash.
item.content:encoded:
The National Transportation Safety Board temporarily pulled its docket system offline after digital images were used to reconstruct cockpit voice recordings of the pilots in a recent crash.

(Image credit: Joel Rose)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: What it means to be a man is a theme in Texas Senate race as Paxton attacks Talarico
item.description: Soon after winning the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff, Ken Paxton attacked Democratic nominee, state Rep. James Talarico as "too low-T for Texas," putting manhood front and center in the race.
item.content:encoded:
Soon after winning the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff, Ken Paxton attacked Democratic nominee, state Rep. James Talarico as "too low-T for Texas," putting manhood front and center in the race.

(Image credit: Danielle Villasana)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben
